arts.ttl: Five challenges ahead for Guardiola and Man City
arts.sum: With Pep Guardiola deciding to stay at Manchester City for another two seasons, BBC Sport looks at the challenges ahead for the club.

arts.ttl: Ange: Spurs have issues, but mentality doesn't worry me
arts.sum: Tottenham have issues, but mentality isn't one of them. That's according to their head coach Ange Postecoglou.

arts.ttl: Manchester United eyeing Atalanta midfielder Ederson for January transfer window
arts.sum: Manchester United are preparing to make a strategic move in the upcoming January transfer window, with Atalanta midfielder Ederson emerging as a prime target for the club. Sky Sport Germany report that the 25-year-old Brazilian has caught the attention of new manager Ruben Amorim as he seeks to strengthen the team’s midfield options. Reports suggest that United are seriously considering Ederson, who has been a standout performer for Atalanta. The midfielder has been a consistent presence in the starting lineup, demonstrating versatility by playing in central and defensive midfield roles. arts.ttl: Harry Kane breaks Erling Haaland Bundesliga record as Bayern Munich beat Augsburg
arts.sum: Harry Kane set a new Bundesliga record on Friday night as Bayern Munich recorded a 3-0 victory over Augsburg at the Allianz Arena.

arts.ttl: Erling Haaland urges Man City to keep calm and carry on amid four-game losing streak... as he sends ominous warning to their Premier League title rivals
arts.sum: It is the first time in boss Pep Guardiola 's career that he has lost four games on the bounce, but it appears there is no panic inside the dressing room, particularly not in the case of Erling Haaland.

arts.ttl: We simulated Man City vs Tottenham as Ange Postecoglou handed huge blow
arts.sum: But, just three points separate the Lilywhites from third place, and Ange Postecoglou will be looking to bounce back to winning ways when they take on Man City later today. We predict that Spurs could line up in a 4-3-3 formation, with their starting XI as follows: Vicario; Porro, Dragusin, Davies, Udogie; Sarr, Bissouma, Kulusevski; Johnson, Solanke, Son.
